title: All RouteIds 1-384 Map to Same Address - Suspicious Hardcoded Logic
reason: `addressAt()` is clearly generated deployment wiring for reserved routes, but this alone does not show an exploitable integrity break or fund-loss path.

title: Delegatecall Allows Arbitrary Code Execution in executeRoute
reason: `executeRoute` is the intended plugin dispatch mechanism; arbitrary execution only follows if the owner installs a malicious route, which is an admin trust assumption rather than a standalone vulnerability.

title: Delegatecall in swapAndMultiBridge Allows Arbitrary Swap Execution
reason: This is the same intended route-dispatch architecture as `executeRoute`, not an independent bug.

title: Unlimited Token Approvals via setApprovalForRouters
reason: Owner-managed approvals to known routers are an explicit admin capability; the claim reduces to owner compromise risk.

title: No Access Control on FeesTakerController Functions
reason: These controller methods are meant to be user-callable helpers and spend the caller's own approvals or value, not shared protocol funds.

title: Unrestricted Refund Function in CelerImpl with No Access Control
reason: `refundCelerUser` validates the refund payload, requires the refund receiver to be the gateway, and forwards funds to the stored original user rather than the caller.

title: Missing Zero Address Validation in addRoute
reason: This is an owner-only misconfiguration footgun with no realistic protocol-level exploit path.

title: Fallback Function Uses msg.sig for Route Routing
reason: The fallback router is an intentional dispatch mechanism; the report does not show an exploitable path beyond the already-reviewed route implementation bugs.

title: No Slippage Protection in Swap Implementations
reason: The 1inch calldata is generated off-chain and already encodes slippage/min-return constraints; this report does not identify a missing on-chain check beyond that design.

title: ERC20 Approvals Not Reset After Bridge Execution
reason: Persistent approvals to trusted external bridge routers are common integration behavior and the claim depends on later compromise of those trusted routers.

title: ExecuteControllers Allows Sequential Arbitrary Delegatecalls
reason: This is the intended controller-composition entrypoint, not a distinct vulnerability.

title: ExecuteRoutes Allows Sequential Arbitrary Delegatecalls
reason: This is the intended route-composition entrypoint, not a distinct vulnerability.

title: Missing Zero Address Validation in addController
reason: This is an owner-only configuration mistake, not a realistic adversarial exploit.

title: SwapAndBridge Uses Delegatecall Without Validation
reason: The bridge contracts are intentionally composing trusted swap routes through the gateway's route table; this is architectural, not a separate bug.

title: Rescue Functions Allow Draining All Funds
reason: These are explicit owner-only rescue powers and therefore part of the admin trust model.

title: Precision Loss in Bridge Ratio Calculation
reason: Integer rounding dust is minor and not a reportable protocol-risk issue compared with the real functional bug in the same flow.

title: Unchecked Return Value of Native Transfer in FeesTakerController
reason: The code checks the boolean result and reverts on failure; the candidate does not describe a real bug.
